sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Check the white cross position before and after
before_seg = before.segmentation['nodes']
after_seg = after.segmentation['nodes']

# Find white/light-gray cross nodes
print("=== BEFORE ===")
for n in before_seg:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'w']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("=== AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'w']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Also check orange/blue bar positions
print("\n=== Orange nodes AFTER ===")
for n in after_seg:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\n=== Blue nodes AFTER (in gray area) ===")
for n in after_seg:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the yellow bar shrank from 84 to 82 pixels (lost 2 pixels from the left side). This confirms it's a timer/step counter. The orange/blue bars moved right when I pressed RIGHT.
So the controls move the orange/blue bar, not the white cross. Let me understand the game:
- The orange/blue bar can be moved with arrow keys
- The yellow bar at the bottom is a timer that shrinks
- The white cross might be a target or starting position
- The black box with blue shapes at the top might be a goal
